stages.name: bindMonetaryColumns output
stages.note: Rule B / total÷qty collapse: 27.30÷2=13.65. Mathematically consistent with qty=2 fiction; unit_price now also differs from PDF gross 27.30.
stages.source: Production replay; .tmp/phase1-validation-forensics-result.json paccheri.lines[0].replay.bound; .tmp/family-a-v25-raw-capture/edge-invoke-final.json (deploy v36 post-bind unit_price=13.65)

stages.name: reconcileLineItemAmounts output
stages.source: Production replay; .tmp/persistence-audit/REPORT.md — reconcile does not alter qty/unit_price/total on audited Bocconcino rows

stages.name: invoice_items persisted (VL DB)
stages.note: Stores pre-bind unit_price (27.36→27.31), not post-bind 13.65. qty×unit_price≠total (2×27.31=54.62≠27.30). First ingest 2026-06-11 held qty=1 (.tmp/vl-final-state-audit/per-invoice/...); current row qty=2 from 2026-06-16 re-ingest.

stages.name: Operational cost calculation
stages.expression: 27.30 total ÷ 6 kg usable (1 case × 6×1 kg from CX 1KG*6 via purchaseQtyForCost=1)

priorStagesCorrect: Pass C era OCR/table GPT qty=1 (.tmp/bocconcino-investigation/extract-invoice-response.json)

downstreamConsistency.bindMonetaryColumns: Given qty=2 and total=27.30, derives unit_price=13.65 (internally consistent; does not restore PDF qty=1)
downstreamConsistency.costAndUI: Split-brain: invoice-line qty=2 drives Last Purchase and bound per-case price; resolveCountablePurchaseQuantityForCost=1 drives usable at one-case volume (6 kg). Usable/op €/kg match PDF 1-case truth by accident; Last Purchase and procurement contradict PDF.

uiVerdict.classificationCaveat: UI audit assumed invoice reality was 2 cases (12 kg) from bound qty=2. True PDF shows qty=1 (1 case, 6 kg). Against PDF: Last Purchase (2 vs 1) and procurement (€13.65 vs €27.30) are wrong; usable 6 kg and €4.55/kg are accidentally correct. User still sees internal contradiction: Last Purchase 2 un vs usable 6 kg (one case only).
uiVerdict.storedVsDisplaySplit: invoice_items stores unit_price=27.31 (pre-bind); detail page displays bound €13.65/case via resolveInvoiceLinePricingPresentation

correctBaselineIfQty1.note: Derived from PDF qty=1 and CX 1KG*6 pack structure; matches current usable/op €/kg but not Last Purchase or procurement display

comparisonWithRicotta.notes: Both Family A Hybrid H qty 1→2 on Bocconcino. Ricotta downstream scales all economics to wrong qty=2 consistently (UI class C vs bound fiction). Mezzi adds multi_unit_pack collapse: purchaseQtyForCost=1 leaves usable at one-case volume while Last Purchase shows doubled invoice qty — split-brain UI (class A).
